response.justification: Bogdan Szabo is a strong full-stack and TypeScript/Node.js engineer, but his profile does not align with the core requirements of this role. His C++ exposure is minimal—roughly 6 months at National Instruments over a decade ago writing components for a C IDE—far short of the mandatory 5–10 years of hands-on C++ on Windows. He has no demonstrable fixed income, derivatives, or capital markets systems experience; his one finance-adjacent role (RIDE Capital) used Node/TypeScript/GraphQL for investment-platform tooling, not trading or fixed-income systems. SQL experience is surface-level (MySQL listed as a skill, no SQL Server or Oracle depth), and his development environment has been Linux-centric throughout his career, not Windows.
strengths: Strong CI/CD and DevOps practices (GitLab, Docker, trunk-based development) across multiple roles
Demonstrated ability to work in high-throughput, low-latency systems (Sociomantic: millions of HTTP requests/second using async I/O)
Java background present, satisfying one optional desirable skill
concerns: C++ experience is minimal (~6 months, 10+ years ago) and nowhere near the required 5–10 years of hands-on Windows C++ development
Zero fixed income, fixed income derivatives, or capital markets domain knowledge; finance exposure is limited to a startup investment-tooling role using a completely different stack
No evidence of SQL Server or Oracle proficiency; Windows platform experience is absent throughout the career history

key_factors.factor: No capital markets / fixed income domain knowledge
